channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Monsoon Session disrupted as Parliament adjourns repeatedly amid large CJP protests over exam paper leaks
item.description: The opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session was marked by major disruptions both inside and outside the House as large-scale protests over alleged examination paper leaks coincided with repeated a...

item.title: Student-led movement demands accountability and reform in India's education system
item.description: A student-led movement in India, known as the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), has grown into a broader campaign for institutional accountability and reform. The movement began after a Supreme Court justi...

item.title: Tanzania's Plan to Boost Kigoma's Economic Potential Through Infrastructure Development
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has unveiled a strategic initiative to transform Kigoma into a major commercial hub, aiming to leverage the region's geographic advantages along Lake Tanganyika. The plan...

item.title: Australia's offshore detention costs in Nauru under scrutiny
item.description: The article examines the financial commitments Australia makes for maintaining offshore detention facilities in Nauru, a Pacific island nation. It highlights the difficulty in tracking exact expenditu...

item.title: Kenyan Entrepreneur Launches KSh 2 Billion Eco-Friendly Residential Project in Nairobi
item.description: Mohamed Bishar, a young Kenyan real estate developer, has officially started construction of the Silva Gigiri residential complex in Nairobi's Gigiri diplomatic area. The KSh 2 billion project, target...

item.title: Sen. Darline Graham to Run for Full Senate Term After Brother's Death
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham (R-South Carolina) has announced her intention to seek a full six-year Senate term following the death of her brother, Lindsey Graham, who passed away on July 11, 2026. South Carol...

item.title: State se apodera de R326 millones en propiedades y vehículos vinculados a sindicatura Maumela
item.description: El Ministerio Público ha ordenado la confiscación definitiva de bienes valorados en aproximadamente R326 millones, incluyendo propiedades de lujo y vehículos de alta gama, pertenecientes a miembros de...

item.title: President Marcos Appoints Lieutenant General Antonio Nafarrete as New AFP Chief
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos has officially appointed Philippine Army commanding general Lieutenant General Antonio Nafarrete as the new chief of staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines ...

item.title: Gujarat man arrested for rape and murder of 4-year-old shot dead by police during custody
item.description: A 30-year-old man, Suresh Vitthal Parmar, was arrested in Gujarat's Anand district on charges of raping and murdering a four-year-old girl. During his transport to the police station, Parmar allegedly...

item.title: Colorado River's Climate and Water Management Crisis
item.description: The article explores the escalating climate crisis affecting the Colorado River, highlighting how the diversion of the river for human use contributes to greenhouse gas emissions, which in turn exacer...

item.title: Nigeria’s energy challenges: From gas abundance to access
item.description: Nigeria, with vast gas reserves, faces a critical gap between supply and demand. Despite producing over 7.5 billion cubic feet of gas daily, distribution infrastructure lags, leaving millions without ...

item.title: Local Dignitaries Attend Pre-Opening Ceremony for Gordie Howe Bridge
item.description: The long-awaited Gordie Howe International Bridge, connecting Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit, Michigan, is set to open on Monday, July 27, 2026. Ahead of this milestone, local dignitaries have been inv...

item.title: Enbridge starts $4 billion natural gas pipeline project in British Columbia
item.description: Enbridge has commenced construction on the Sunrise Expansion Program, a $4 billion initiative to expand its West Coast Natural Gas pipeline system. The project, which began with a ceremony in Prince G...

item.title: Durban authorities target crime-ridden building in CBD crackdown
item.description: A multi-departmental operation was launched in Durban's Central Business District to address a building identified as a major crime generator. The operation, conducted on Lancers Road near health faci...

item.title: TISM's satirical approach to interviews and their enduring cult status
item.description: The article explores the Australian band TISM's unique approach to interviews, where they blend satire and self-deprecation to engage with their fans. Formed in the 1980s, TISM is known for their prov...

item.title: Unsolved Edmonton Murder of Nurse Nadine Robinson Still Lacks Closure After 20 Years
item.description: Nadine Robinson, a 36-year-old nurse from Edmonton, was found dead in her townhouse in 2006. Her death remains unsolved, with no charges filed against any suspect. Her son, Stephen Robinson, expressed...

item.title: Moshi Region Faces Land Use Conflict Between Livestock Farmers and Agricultural Owners
item.description: Authorities in Moshi have issued a stern warning to livestock keepers in the region, cautioning them against allowing their animals to trespass onto agricultural land as tensions between herders and c...

item.title: FG Denies Defence Minister's Resignation Rumours
item.description: The Federal Government has officially denied reports suggesting that Defence Minister General Christopher Musa (retd.) is planning to resign. A statement released by the Minister of Defence's office c...

item.title: Spanish Soccer Star's Viral Handshake With Trump Sparks Global Reaction Amid FIFA World Cup
item.description: The FIFA World Cup has become a global stage for both sporting excellence and political intrigue. Lamine Yamal, the young Spanish soccer prodigy, recently made headlines not for his on-field performan...

item.title: US Condemns China's Aggressive Actions in South China Sea Clash with Philippine Navy
item.description: The United States has condemned China's 'dangerous and aggressive' actions during a recent incident in the South China Sea, where Chinese Coast Guard personnel allegedly injured a Filipino sailor. The...
